athletes.name: Sean Helmot
athletes.training_philosophy: HR-primary, pace secondary. Aerobic base first, sharpen closer to race. Easy runs and long runs Z1-Z2 HR. Quality 1-2x per week. Long run progressive HR blocks.

typical_week.Wed: Quality session (threshold reps or intervals, 10-12km total)
typical_week.Thu: Easy run + drills, gym legs PM (8km Z1-Z2 HR)
typical_week.Fri: Easy run + flat strides (9-10km Z1-Z2 HR)
typical_week.Sat: Easy run — pre-LR activation (6-8km Z1-Z2 HR)

upcoming.name: Gold Coast Marathon
upcoming.description:
Gold Coast Marathon

Block 1 - Patience
- 7km 6:15-6:20/km Pace

Block 2 - Rhythm
- 7km 6:10-6:15/km Pace

Block 3 - Cruise
- 7km 6:05-6:10/km Pace

Block 4 - Race
- 7km 6:00-6:05/km Pace

Block 5 - Commit
- 7km 5:55-6:00/km Pace

Block 6 - Empty the tank
- 7.2km 5:45-5:55/km Pace

KEY: First 7km split 44:05-44:40. Halfway: 2:09-2:12. 

First gel km 5, then every 5km: km 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35 — seven gels total. If any are caffeinated, save them for km 25 onwards. Nothing new on race day — whatever you've been training with.
Water at every station. You're a salty sweater so take the Gatorade on course where it's available rather than water only — it'll cover your electrolytes across the race. If you have electrolyte tabs or salt caps you've used in training, take one at km 15 and one at km 30.


-- Coach Helmot

upcoming.name: Cross Training — 30min
upcoming.description:
Phase: Post-Marathon Recovery — Week 1

- 30m easy cross training

OPTIONAL — rest is completely fine today too. If you feel good and want to move, aqua jog, pool walk, or easy bike are all zero-impact options. Keep it very easy. Follow with 15 min full-body stretch. Coach Helmot.

upcoming.name: Run/Walk — 25min
upcoming.description:
Phase: Post-Marathon Recovery — Week 1

- 5m Walk

5x
- 2m 60-70% HR
- 1m Walk

- 5m Walk

OPTIONAL — pure rest is still completely appropriate this week. Only attempt this if legs feel genuinely ready. If in doubt, walk the whole thing or skip it entirely. No ego. Coach Helmot.

upcoming.name: 5x Mile @ CV w/90s SR
upcoming.description:
Main Set

5x
- 1 mi 3:48-3:53/km Pace
- 90s Rest

CV effort -- controlled and consistent across all 5 reps. Rep 1 should feel almost easy. If you're fighting to hold pace by rep 3, you went out too hard. Coach Helmot.

athletes.name: Aidan Burrell
athletes.training_philosophy: Inexperienced runner building base fitness. Conservative pacing, HR-capped easy runs. Focus on consistency and injury prevention.

upcoming.description:
Phase: Base Building — Week 1

- 5m Z2 Pace
- 40m 5:05-5:20/km Pace
- 5m Z2 Pace

Comfortably hard in the main block — you can speak in sentences but not chat freely. Coach Helmot.

upcoming.name: Long Run — 80min
upcoming.description:
Phase: Base Building — Week 1

- 80m 5:30-5:45/km Pace

Run by time. Walk uphills if needed. Time on feet is the goal today. Coach Helmot.

upcoming.name: Easy Run — 40min
upcoming.description:
Phase: Base Building — Week 2

- 40m 5:30-5:45/km Pace

Easy throughout. Notice how your body feels compared to last week — that is adaptation. Coach Helmot.

upcoming.name: Moderate Run — 50min
upcoming.description:
Phase: Base Building — Week 2

- 5m Z2 Pace
- 40m 5:05-5:20/km Pace
- 5m Z2 Pace

Try to hold the main block pace comfortably throughout. If it feels too easy, trust the process. Coach Helmot.
